programme.title: The Wall

She then tries to impress the bride-to-be's mother during a dinner date, but is so nervous she turns to drink, and the evening descends into chaos. However, the streetwise kid refuses to give evidence unless his own and his family's safety is guaranteed, and hires a lawyer to ensure his demands are met. programme.desc: The team explores Wembury in Devon, with Anita Rani learning about its naval history, Joe Crowley investigating its rock pools as he takes part in an annual nature survey and Ellie Harrison going in search of its marine wildlife. Elsewhere, Adam Henson is moving some of his sheep and Tom Heap is on the trail of the ramraiding gangs ripping out the ATMs on which villages rely(n)

programme.title: Strictly Come Dancing

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team are in Morden Hall Park in south London, where the experts asses items including a Faberge vanity case, a ring by one of the most innovative goldsmiths of the 20th century and a magnifying glass linked to the Pablo Picasso exhibition in New York in 1939. Lennox Cato faces a puzzle when he appraises a pair of antique doors belonging to the local mosque and jewellery specialist Jon Benjamin gets his hands on the largest aquamarine he has ever seen(n)

programme.title: World on Fire
programme.desc: After enlisting in the Royal Navy, Tom finds himself on board the HMS Exeter in pursuit of German pocket battleship the Graf Spee, and Tom watches the carnage and death around him as a huge naval battle ensues in the South Atlantic. Harry crosses paths with Lois again when she arrives to perform at the BEF base camp in France, while Robina steps in to defend Jan in the playground. In Berlin, Nancy is shocked by rumours of sick children being forcefully taken to clinics and killed and decides to investigate for herself(n)

programme.title: Hindus: Do We Have a Caste Problem?
programme.desc: Parle Patel investigates the hereditary class system of Hindu society, asking what kind of impact does it have on other Hindus living in Britain. He meets a young woman who is so proud of her caste she is still working in the same occupation as her ancestors did thousands of years ago, and also speaks to a man who has rejected his religion because he believes it's the only way to escape his caste(n)

programme.title: The Women's Football Show

programme.desc: Carolina Panthers v Tampa Bay Buccaneers. Action from the NFC South clash, as Tottenham Hotspur Stadium hosted a match for the second successive week(n)

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: England cricketer Joe Denly, former striker Chris Sutton, Great Britain sprinter Bianca Williams and Warrington Wolves winger Josh Charnley join team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Dan Walker and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.title: Rip Off Britain: Live
programme.desc: Angela Rippon, Gloria Hunniford and Julia Somerville return with a ive week of the consumer series, reporting on people who have lost tens of thousands of pounds in dormant accounts that banks say are untraceable(n)

programme.title: Caught Red Handed
programme.desc: Dominic Littlewood returns to reveal more of the innovative methods that police officers and members of the public are using to catch criminals in the act. A neighbourly dispute gets out of hand, a shop worker fears for his life when two violent men burst in, and a thief goes into a pet shop and steals a puppy(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Charlie Ross presents from the Royal Cornwall Showground in Wadebridge as Thomas Forrester and Tim Weeks help two teams look for items to sell at auction in Plymouth(n)

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Imogen struggles to open up about her health fears, Ruhma bares her soul on camera, and Sid finds the key to helping a patient lies in a controversial painting and a family secret(n)

programme.title: Head Hunters
programme.desc: Rob Beckett hosts the quiz in which contestants must choose between working together to combine their knowledge or going it alone to claim the prize for themselves(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Perfect Town
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin puts Tunbridge Wells and its property market to the test with his first- time buyers, who have £425,000 to invest in a starter home after saving hard for the deposit(n)

programme.title: Make Me a Dealer
programme.desc: Paul Martin is in Dorset as driving instructor Helen Adams and former flight attendant Yvonne Johnson use their own cash to buy and sell antiques to try to make the most profit(n)

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore salvages three items from a recycling centre in Witley, with Leigh-Anne Treadwell and Neil Wragg helping transform a sofa, some deckchairs and two tables(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Angellica Bell and Matt Baker present the first visit of the week to the One Show studio, featuring the usual mix of topical stories and celebrity chat(n)

programme.title: Inside Out London
programme.desc: Tom Edwards finds out why HS2 project has been halted, while Afua Adom reveals how the shared ownership dream can turn into a nightmare. Sean Fletcher presents(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Kathy suggests to Max that she could supervise while Rainie babysits Abi - but Stuart has a more devious plan up his sleeve. Gray offers to treat Chantelle to an overnight stay in a hotel, while Tiffany tells Keegan she is ready to take their relationship to the next level. Louise and Bex have a heart to heart and Sonia breaks down while talking through her problems with Lisa(n)

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: The Government has promised Britain will radically reduce carbon emissions to achieve net-zero emissions by 2050. To get there, people need to make big changes to the way they live, but how much difference will they really make? With the help of a family in Nottingham, Justin Rowlatt finds out what are the most important things people can do and asks what the government is doing to encourage everyone to alter the way they live(n)

programme.title: Dublin Murders
programme.desc: In the summer of 2006, detectives Rob Reilly and Cassie Maddox are sent to investigate a child's murder, and find a community caught between the old and the new Ireland. Psychological crime drama based on the Dublin Murder Squad book series by Tana French, starring Killian Scott and Sarah Greene, with Ned Dennehy, Conleth Hill and Tom Vaughan-Lawlor(n)

programme.title: The Young Offenders
programme.desc: After the disastrous encounter at the Walsh family barbecue, Conor and Jock reason that there is only one option left to get Linda and Siobhan to really fall for them - spend a whole day together on a beautiful adventure. Meanwhile, Mairead's plan to invite a 'friend' over for dinner doesn't go down well with Conor. Comedy, starring Alex Murphy and Chris Walley(n)

programme.title: Have I Got a Bit More News for You
programme.desc: Doc Martin star Martin Clunes hosts an extended edition of the satirical quiz, with two panellists joining regular team captains Ian Hislop and Paul Merton to poke fun at the week's events(n)

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: Among the guests on the sofa are singer-songwriter Bruce Springsteen, who talks about his new documentary Western Stars, Oscar winner Robert De Niro, starring in Martin Scorsese's The Irishman, and actor and comedian Paul Rudd, who discusses his new TV series Living with Yourself. Music is supplied by James Blunt, who performs Cold, from his new album(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: Ali-A's Superchargers
programme.desc: A sporty brother and sister from Wales modify their family car(n)

programme.title: Top Class
programme.desc: Two teams take on the challenge of answering questions(n)

programme.title: Mustangs FC
programme.desc: Liv questions whether soccer is really her passion(n)

programme.title: Blue Peter
programme.desc: Long-running children's magazine programme(n)

programme.title: Turbo (2013)
programme.desc: A garden snail obsessed with motor racing gets sucked into a car's exhaust pipe, and emerges having gained super-speed. This stroke of luck allows him to realise his dream of competing against human drivers in the prestigious Indianapolis 500 race. Animated adventure, with the voices of Ryan Reynolds, Paul Giamatti and Samuel L Jackson(n)

programme.title: Robot Wars
programme.desc: Dara O Briain and Angela Scanlon present another round of battles in the arena. An untested robot built by a cardiologist and his two sons makes a surprising impact in the arena, while a builder from Northampton wheels out an impressive looking axe-wielding robot. Plus, judge Sethu Vijayakumar discusses his work with Nasa, designing robots to explore Mars(n)

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Tennis coach Judy Murray and former British number one Greg Rusedski compete, with expert guidance coming from Angus Ashworth and Raj Bisram(n)

programme.title: Hairy Bikers: Route 66
programme.desc: For the fourth leg of their journey, Dave Myers and Si King travels the 400 miles from Amarillo, Texas to Albuquerque in New Mexico. In Amarillo, the pair visit the oldest burger joint in the city, before riding to Palo Duro Canyon to explore a ghost town. Finally arriving in Albuquerque, they join residents to watch a game at the local ballpark and bake a chili chocolate pie to celebrate their cross-country adventure(n)

programme.title: The Best of British Takeaways
programme.desc: Chef Tom Kerridge tries to find out what makes the best Indian curry and uncovers the trade secrets behind our takeaways, while Cherry Healey explores the fascinating stories that helped shape our communities as well as our palates, and learns how Queen Victoria put curry on the British menu. Three award-winning Indian takeaways are then invited to compete in a contest in Bradford, each with their own ideas as to what makes the best takeaway, with Enam Ali acting as judge to decide the winner(n)

programme.title: Best House in Town
programme.desc: Three much-loved detached properties in Cardiff compete to be chosen for the final - a modern house with sea views, a country cottage, and a renovated farmhouse(n)

programme.title: Your Home Made Perfect
programme.desc: Helen and Rob live in a Victorian terraced house in St Albans. They have put all their energy into renovating the entrance and the front room, but the back of the house remains a mishmash of tiny and unpractical rooms. Architects Robert Jamison and Laura Jane Clark compete to take control of this renovation project. Angela Scanlon presents(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin helps an entrepreneurial couple with a £650,000 budget find their dream home in the Wye Valley, and also visits the UK's first solar aquaponics greenhouse(n)

programme.title: James May's Cars of the People
programme.desc: James looks at some of the advances in motoring that never caught on, revealing how the petrol engine came to dominate in the 20th century, while concepts for jet engines and steam power fell by the wayside. He also drives an amphibious car and an electric scooter, and takes a ride in the world's fastest driverless car(n)

programme.title: Live Gymnastics
programme.desc: Matt Baker presents further live coverage of the penultimate day from Hanns Martin Schleyer Halle in Stuttgart, Germany, featuring the men's pommel horse. Great Britain's Max Whitlock is the reigning Olympic champion in this discipline, but was narrowly beaten into silver by China's Xiao Ruoteng in this event last year. With analysis from Beth Tweddle, and commentary by Christine Still and Craig Heap(n)

programme.title: River Walks
programme.desc: Former Coronation Street star Shobna Gulati takes a stroll through Shropshire and Worcestershire along the banks of the Severn, Great Britain's longest river, finding out about a part of the country that is a world away from her native north-west England(n)

programme.title: What Britain Buys and Sells in a Day
programme.desc: The team looks at the seafood trade, with Ed Balls finding out how the cargo hub at Heathrow is a crucial link in Britain's supply chain and going behind the scenes of the airport operations centre to witness how delays can affect it. Cherry Healey is in the Highlands to find out why the world is so fond of salmon farmed there and fishes for langoustine on Loch Fyne, while Ade Adepitan travels to Iceland to catch one of Britain's favourite type of fish and unravels the story of the Cod Wars(n)

programme.title: Dad's Army
programme.desc: Captain Mainwaring camps the platoon in a deserted barn, unaware it is inside the target range of an artillery exercise. Only Corporal Jones knows the danger, leading to a desperate race to raise the alarm in time. Clive Dunn, Arthur Lowe and John Le Mesurier star in an episode first shown in November 1972(n)

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.desc: Romesh Ranganathan comperes an evening of stand-up at London's Hammersmith Apollo, treating the audience to his own brand of humour before introducing routines by comedy circuit stars Jason Byrne and Stewart Francis(n)

programme.title: Mark Ronson: From the Heart
programme.desc: The songwriter and producer discusses his life and musical influences, including how the break-up of his marriage has influenced his most recent album, Late Night Feelings. The programme follows Ronson in London, Los Angeles and New York at work and at home, and there are interviews with family and friends, along with the likes of Lady Gaga, Simon Le Bon, Josh Homme and Bradley Cooper(n)

programme.title: Tron: Legacy (2010)
programme.desc: Ground-breaking computer genius Kevin Flynn disappears without trace. Years later, his son Sam goes looking for him, and is transported into a computer-generated world where he is forced to compete in deadly gladiatorial combat by an evil duplicate of his father plotting to wage war on the real world. Sci-fi adventure, with Jeff Bridges and Garrett Hedlund(n)

programme.title: Valhalla Rising (2009)
programme.desc: A mysterious mute warrior leads a life of slavery under a brutal tribal chieftain, but manages to kill his master and escape. Joining a band of Vikings who have embraced Christianity, he is recruited into a mission to conquer Jerusalem. Historical adventure, starring Mads Mikkelsen, Maarten Stevenson and Jamie Sives(n)

programme.title: The Name of the Rose
programme.desc: In 1327, an enlightened Franciscan friar, William of Baskerville, and his young apprentice investigate a series of mysterious deaths at an abbey, risking the wrath of a powerful Inquisitor who has been sent by the Pope himself. John Turturro and Rupert Everett star in the latest adaptation of Umberto Eco's religious thriller, along with Damian Hardung, Michael Emerson and James Cosmo(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: Life in a Cottage Garden with Carol Klein
programme.desc: In July and August, the horticulturist deadheads the roses and stakes plants to help keep the garden looking its best during the summer months, and savours the full blooms of geraniums and lilies. She creates colourful borders using cannas, ginger and dahlias, before visiting her local beach, Braunton Burrows, to seek out native sea holly, which she hopes to grow herself(n)

programme.title: James Wong and the Malaysian Garden
programme.desc: Ethnobotanist and garden designer James Wong travels to Malaysia in search of inspiration for his Chelsea Flower Show entry. He travels across the country meeting people who live in traditional communities among the city skyscrapers and discovers how they use plants for medicine and ceremony. He also travels to Bako National Park in Sarawak to look for an elusive pitcher plant, before travelling back to the UK to put his design together in time for the horticultural event(n)

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Monty Don gives tips on moving plants which are in the wrong place or have outgrown their space, shows how to store chillies and harvests the last of his tomatoes. Rachel de Thame heads to Longmeadow to talk to Monty about her return to health and to plant up pots for spring colour, while Nick Bailey travels to the Highlands in Scotland to find out about a project which is contributing to worldwide plant conservation(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Helen Skelton is in Buckinghamshire to learn about the history of the harvest festival and the traditions surrounding it - from the introduction of the threshing machine to the origins of the corn dolly. There are also films from the programme's archive about harvests, including Matt Baker finding out about Jersey Royal potatoes still being unearthed in the same way they were 200 years ago(n)

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Best Bites
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt presents highlights from throughout the series, offering viewers a chance to revisit some of their favourite moments from previous years in the kitchen(n)

programme.title: Live Gymnastics
programme.desc: Matt Baker presents live coverage of the final day from Hanns Martin Schleyer Halle in Stuttgart, Germany, featuring the men's and women's apparatus finals. All eyes will be on America's Simone Biles once again as she aims for a fifth women's floor title, while Dutchman Epke Zonderland is among the star attractions in the men's disciplines as he goes for a fourth high bar gold. With analysis from Beth Tweddle, and commentary by Christine Still and Craig Heap(n)

programme.title: Seven Wonders of the Commonwealth
programme.desc: To celebrate the start of the Commonwealth Games, five presenters travel the globe to reveal natural beauty spots in seven of the Commonwealth's 53 countries. Dan Snow kayaks alongside bottlenose dolphins in Milford Sound, New Zealand, and travels to Bangladesh to visit the world's largest mangrove forest. Anita Rani travels to the oldest, driest desert on earth, the Namib in Africa, where temperatures can reach 50 degrees centigrade - then experiences tribal life in Papua New Guinea. Reggie Yates journeys to the largest uninhabited island in the Solomons, surrounded by pristine coral reefs, Denise Lewis experiences the Zambia's Victoria Falls, the most spectacular waterfall on Earth, and Clare Balding heads to the Inner Hebrides to retrace the footsteps of composer Felix Mendelssohn, who was inspired by the volcanic Fingal's Cave on Staffa(n)

programme.title: The Super League Show
programme.desc: St Helens v Salford Red Devils. Action from the showpiece match at Old Trafford, which saw Salford compete in the Grand Final for the first time in their history(n)

programme.title: Saving Lives at Sea
programme.desc: A volunteer in Scotland has to rescue his own father, whose fishing boat is drifting powerless towards a rocky shoreline. In Bristol, a father and son are among the crew that comes to the aid of two five-year-old girls who have drifted into the Bristol channel on an inflatable, while in Ilfracombe, lifeboat volunteers race against time to save a mother and her daughter stranded at the base of huge sea cliffs(n)

programme.title: Africa with Ade Adepitan
programme.desc: The presenter explores the continent, and examines how African nations may influence international affairs in the next 50 years as life expectancy, population, and economic growth rise. He starts by travelling from Cape Verde to his birthplace in Nigeria, passing through Senegal and Ivory Coast along the way. He discovers how a region once ravaged by the slave trade is still being exploited by Western companies, who dominate the fishing trade, plays a game of para soccer on the streets of Lagos and works with salt collectors at Senegal's Lake Retba(n)

programme.title: Dragons' Den
programme.desc: Evan Davis hosts the business ideas challenge, which sees vitamin company CEO Tej Lalvani and cash machine tycoon Jenny Campbell joining returning Dragons Deborah Meaden, Peter Jones and Touker Suleyman to cast an eye over more money-making schemes - with the largest investments in the show's history up for grabs. The first batch of entrepreneurs braving the den present their ideas for an allergy-friendly food range, artificial grass for schools and youth centres, and a voucher code website that could revolutionise online shopping(n)

programme.title: The Americas with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: Simon reaches the United States, travelling down the Rocky Mountains from Montana to Colorado. In the wide-open prairies of Montana, the cowboy way of life is under threat from a long-term decline in the rural economy, but Simon meets a former Silicon Valley executive who has envisaged a new future for the land. In a country with the highest incarceration rate in the world, Simon's final destination on this leg is a Colorado town that depends almost entirely on 11 prisons. Simon witnesses how inmates are put to work for the state, and how virtual reality is being used to prepare prisoners for life on the outside(n)

programme.title: The Mash Report
programme.desc: Nish Kumar and a cast of comedy correspondents including Ellie Taylor and Steve N Allen present the satirical and surreal take on the week's news(n)

programme.title: Love in the Countryside
programme.desc: Sara Cox delivers a collection of love letters to George, a 24-year-old vet in Hay-on-Wye who has not had a relationship in more than two years. She helps him to arrange a speed dating evening with the writers of his favourite letters - but meeting them face to face proves nerve-racking. Dairy farmer David invites three potential partners to stay with him in Cumbria so they can get a taste of rural life. Plus, an update on Martin and Grace, to see how they are getting on in their dating journeys(n)

programme.title: The Spectacular Now (2013)
programme.desc: A teenage loner wakes up one morning to find a classmate passed out drunk on her lawn. They form an unlikely friendship, and he offers her help in constructing a new persona for herself that will boost her confidence - but begins to fall in love with the act she is putting on. Drama based on the novel by Tim Tharp, starring Miles Teller, Shailene Woodley and Brie Larson(n)

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the topical debate from Beckenham in Bromley, Greater London, inviting the studio audience to question a panel of politicians and other guests on subjects that have made the headlines over the past week(n)

programme.title: Best House in Town
programme.desc: Homeowners across the UK open their doors to five property-obsessed neighbours in the hope of being judged as having the outstanding house in their locality, beginning in York. In the first edition, the owners of a modern penthouse, a split-level flat, a new-build and a Georgian flat try to reach this week's final(n)

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Ric tries to keep his diagnosis - and the life-or-death decision it has presented him with - a secret from Darla, who is wrestling with a tough choice of her own. Essie is confronted by Isla's angry father, who demands to know the identity of her real mother and threatens to expose the illegal adoption. Kian discovers one of his patients is an old flame, but their unresolved feelings for each other may threaten his judgement(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore scours the recycling centre in Witley for items she can breathe new life into, including a tumble dryer drum and a teak record cabinet(n)

programme.title: Fugitives
programme.desc: A two-year search for a man who abandoned kilos of cocaine at a North Yorkshire airfield ends when the fugitive turns up, soaking wet, on a beach in Gibraltar(n)

programme.title: Saving Lives at Sea
programme.desc: A volunteer in Scotland has to rescue his own father, whose fishing boat is drifting powerless towards a rocky shoreline. In Bristol, a father and son are among the crew that comes to the aid of two five-year-old girls who have drifted into the Bristol channel on an inflatable, while in Ilfracombe, lifeboat volunteers race against time to save a mother and her daughter stranded at the base of huge sea cliffs(n)

programme.title: Victoria Derbyshire
programme.desc: Daily news and current-affairs programme offering discussion of breaking stories, exclusive interviews and audience interaction via social media(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A couple tell Martin Roberts the plans for their Stoke-on-Trent property and Martel Maxwell is in Leeds with a first-time developer. Dion Dublin views a Merseyside property(n)

programme.title: Claimed and Shamed
programme.desc: Ore Oduba returns with the programme exposing cases of insurance fraud, including a man who tried to make the same personal injury claim three times in 12 years(n)

programme.title: Close Calls: On Camera
programme.desc: Nick Knowles returns to introduce more footage of near misses caught on CCTV, featuring a driver opening his car door and sending a young motorcyclist into the path of a bus and a mother-to-be who calls 999 after suffering massive blood loss(n)

programme.title: BBC Newsroom Live
programme.desc: A chance to stay up to date on the day's leading stories, with the latest breaking news as it happens(n)

programme.title: The Boss
programme.desc: Quiz hosted by Susan Calman in which one contestant becomes the Boss and has to use judgement and tactics to decide which player is best suited to answer questions(n)

programme.title: My Life on a Plate
programme.desc: Brian Turner invites veteran broadcaster and entertainer Nicholas Parsons to delve into his youth in Hampstead Heath. The chef cooks nostalgic dishes in an outdoor kitchen, hoping to transport Nicholas back to his mother's cooking and reflect the kind of food he likes eating now(n)

programme.title: Emergency Rescue Down Under
programme.desc: British emergency workers at the other side of the world. A cop deals with trouble at an Australian rules football game and a flying doctor is called out to a freak accident in the Bush(n)

programme.title: Heir Hunters
programme.desc: A woman has an emotional reunion with her niece after being estranged for 30 years, while another case reveals links to 19th-century Whitechapel and the Jack the Ripper murders(n)

programme.title: The Wonder of Animals
programme.desc: Chris Packham explores the anatomy and physiology of the largest land animal on the planet, the elephant. Its size seems ill-suited to surviving the most arid regions of Africa, but its inner workings allow it to defy the extreme heat of the desert, and find food and water in seemingly barren landscapes, while its extraordinary memory enables it to repel predators. Here, Chris reveals how hairs on the skin help to keep the elephant cool, and how sensors in its feet may be able to guide it toward rain(n)

programme.title: Yellowstone
programme.desc: Kate Humble, Patrick Ayree, scientists and wildlife cameramen examine the changing seasons at Yellowstone National Park during 2016, and the effect on the local American wildlife. In the first episode, Patrick follows the grizzly bears, who are taking a risk with the weather by leaving their winter dens early. Meanwhile, Kate gets to grips with the science behind this frozen season(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Elizabeth Talbot and Paul Laidlaw hunt for antiques in the Bowes Museum at Barnard Castle in Co Durham, with finds including a silver tea set and a Russian drinking cup. Plus, Paul Martin looks at the museum's extraordinary lace collection(n)

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Angellica Bell, Gyles Brandreth, Scarlett Moffatt and Iain Stirling test their general knowledge skills in a variety of entertaining trivia-based games this week(n)

programme.title: Strictly: It Takes Two
programme.desc: Rylan Clark-Neal is joined by the latest couple to be eliminated from the competition after losing the dance-off at the weekend. Plus, interviews with the remaining pairs(n)

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Red Dwarf co-stars Craig Charles and Robert Llewellyn travel around the West Midlands in search of antiques, assisted by experts Izzie Balmer and Tim Medhurst. Robert is smitten with a 200-year-old love letter, while Craig splurges on a multitude of quirky curios(n)

programme.title: Only Connect
programme.desc: Victoria Coren Mitchell hosts another first-round match as Inspector Morse fans the Endeavours take on mathematicians the Outliers. The teams must use patience, lateral thinking and sheer inspiration to make connections between four things that may appear at first not to be linked, with one set of clues consisting of FedEx [east], Volvo [north east], Amazon [A to Z] and Recycling [in a circle](n)

programme.title: University Challenge
programme.desc: The academic quiz's first-round matches continue, with four students from Wolfson College, Oxford pitting their wits against a team from the University of Sheffield in a bid to reach the second stage of the competition. Jeremy Paxman asks the questions(n)

programme.title: What Britain Buys and Sells in a Day
programme.desc: The team looks at the automotive trade, with Ed Balls at the Port of Southampton to see how cars are driven off and on vast vessels at speed and witnessing the exhaustive checks that BMW cars must go through before they reach their owners Cherry Healey visits a Toyota factory in Derbyshire to see how workers turn millions of imported parts a day into new cars, while Ade Adepitan travels to the Tesla Gigafactory in Nevada to see how their batteries are made and what the future holds for British car manufacturing(n)

programme.title: Motherland
programme.desc: Having resigned from her job in PR, Julia tries to adapt to life as a freelancer and reluctantly agrees to help Amanda launch her new shop - even though she believes it is nothing but a vanity project. The opening is a success, but a careless online post threatens to destroy her good work. Meanwhile, Liz rails against the gentrification of the area and the loss of her beloved kebab shop, while Kevin reveals he has written an unusual children's book, which he wants Amanda to sell in her shop(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events. Presented by Emily Maitlis(n)

programme.title: The Americas with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: Simon reaches the United States, travelling down the Rocky Mountains from Montana to Colorado. In the wide-open prairies of Montana, the cowboy way of life is under threat from a long-term decline in the rural economy, but Simon meets a former Silicon Valley executive who has envisaged a new future for the land. In a country with the highest incarceration rate in the world, Simon's final destination on this leg is a Colorado town that depends almost entirely on 11 prisons. Simon witnesses how inmates are put to work for the state, and how virtual reality is being used to prepare prisoners for life on the outside(n)

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: Bill Bailey narrates this natural history documentary telling the story of some of nature's most extraordinary partnerships, revealing what sometimes happens when animals need an itch scratching, help getting into food or home security assistance. The answer is, to find a partner - and not necessarily one of the same species. Highlights include hippos joining forces with fish for a full-body exfoliation and ravens inviting wolverines over for some bone-crunching assistance with their dinner(n)

programme.title: Wild China
programme.desc: The documentary series exploring China's wildlife continues with a journey to the Yunnan Province, where parallel mountain ranges channel mighty rivers from Tibet southward to the tropics, and the landscape provides a home to diverse species. While the mountain forests are home to red pandas, the valleys below contain jungles inhabited by gibbons, sunbirds and elephants(n)

programme.title: Spiral
programme.desc: Commissioner Herville is found dead in a Chinese restaurant in the 13th arrondissement of Paris, and Gilou must break the news to Laure, who is being treated in a police rehabilitation centre. Gilou, now the head of the unit, must investigate with a new partner, fresh out of training. The return of the French detective drama, starring Caroline Proust, Gregory Fitoussi, Philippe Duclos and Thierry Godard(n)

programme.title: Spiral
programme.desc: Gilou agrees to work with Laure again and they set out to find Ryan and his gang, while Edelman does everything he can to get Josephine out of prison. In French(n)

programme.title: Classic Albums
programme.desc: The making of Back to Black, the second and final studio album by singer-songwriter Amy Winehouse, which was released in October 2006 to wide critical and commercial acclaim. Featuring singles Rehab, You Know I'm No Good, Back to Black, Tears Dry on Their Own and Love Is a Losing Game, the record has sold almost four million copies and is currently 13th in the list of best-selling albums in the UK(n)

programme.title: Classic Albums
programme.desc: The story behind Nirvana's 1991 album Nevermind, which catapulted the Seattle trio from relative obscurity to superstardom. It examines the effect their music had on the record industry and the band themselves, and provides a glimpse into frontman Kurt Cobain's acclaimed songwriting skills. Featuring contributions by bassist Krist Novoselic, drummer Dave Grohl and producer Butch Vig(n)

programme.title: Classic Albums
programme.desc: Concert footage, home movies and interviews in Jamaica give a background to the Wailers' LP Catch a Fire, which launched Bob Marley onto the global music scene. Record label boss Chris Blackwell reveals how he masterminded the overdubbing of the album for the rock market and Rabbit Brundrick (keyboards) and Wayne Perkins (electric guitar) tell how they were brought back in to add their parts to the songs(n)

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1988
programme.desc: Janice Long and Mark Goodier present the pop chart programme, first broadcast on 4 August 1988. Featuring Kim Wilde, The Funky Worm and Fairground Attraction(n)

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: Bill Bailey narrates this natural history documentary telling the story of some of nature's most extraordinary partnerships, revealing what sometimes happens when animals need an itch scratching, help getting into food or home security assistance. The answer is, to find a partner - and not necessarily one of the same species. Highlights include hippos joining forces with fish for a full-body exfoliation and ravens inviting wolverines over for some bone-crunching assistance with their dinner(n)

programme.title: The Women's Football Show
programme.desc: Highlights of the weekend's games in the Super League, and a look at the biggest issues in the women's game. Arsenal are the defending champions, but face stiff competition from the likes of Chelsea, Manchester City and newly promoted Manchester United(n)

programme.title: Handmade in Bolton
programme.desc: Oxford historian Dr Janina Ramirez sets reformed forger Shaun Greenhalgh his hardest task yet - to carve an Islamic bottle out of rock crystal in the style of the 10th-century Egyptian Fatimids. Rock crystal is notoriously fragile and sourcing the right quantities of it is almost impossible, but the real problems begin when the carving is finished(n)

programme.title: Britain's Biggest Warship
programme.desc: HMS Queen Elizabeth goes on dangerous sea trials for the first time in the North Sea, where every part of the vessel is put to the test and the first aircraft lands on the deck. After a few days at sea, the first problems start to emerge, including an explosion, a dangerous fuel leak, and some strange noises. Meanwhile, a crew member reveals how the Navy helped him make a fresh start from a life of crime, and the ship's Muslim chef works out how to cook pork without offending his religion(n)

programme.title: The Sky at Night
programme.desc: In this hour-long special, the Sky at Night team faces a live studio audience to answer their questions about the mysteries and wonders of the universe(n)

programme.title: France 1939: One Last Summer
programme.desc: A collection of home movies, diaries and letters from the last summer before the start of the Second World War reveal how far the impending conflict was away from the minds of ordinary French people. The film describes the final weeks of peace in France and the mix of blindness, denial and prophetic clear-sightedness of those facing the war that was about to unfold(n)

programme.title: Britain's Greatest Generation
programme.desc: The Second World War saw men from all classes unite to fight the Nazis, and this programme meets some of the last surviving pilots of the Battle of Britain. Women, who had been excluded from many jobs, worked in everything from anti-aircraft gun batteries to steel-making factories. The Blitz saw rescue workers risk all to save those trapped in bombed buildings. For many of the era's last survivors, the defiant spirit that brought the nation together remains one of their proudest moments, as this documentary reveals(n)

programme.title: The Sinner
programme.desc: Ambrose unearths a startling secret about Cora that changes the course of the investigation, while Cora begs Mason to bring Laine to visit her(n)

programme.title: The Story of Skinhead with Don Letts
programme.desc: The director and 6Music presenter offers a first-hand account of the skinhead movement, tracing its origins in the 1960s as a 'harmonious subgroup' through to its evolution throughout the 1970s and 1980s into a 'threatening and bigoted subculture steeped in far-right politics and violence'. Don reflects on the movement's social impact across music, style and culture, its modern-day legacy, and its associated stigma associated by exploring white power nationalists in the US, fascist revivalists in Italy and Neo-Nazis in Germany. Contributors include singer Pauline Black(n)

programme.title: Beats, Bass and Bars - The Story of Grime
programme.desc: Almost 20 years on from its first beginnings, grime dominates the music charts and awards ceremonies, and even influences politics. Here, rapper Rodney P examines how grime rose from the council estates of east London to become the most important British musical movement since punk. He discovers that its success rests upon the original styles and contributions of previous generations of artists and learns that grime can only be truly understood when viewed as part of a broader social narrative and ever-evolving musical culture that goes back to the 1980s(n)

programme.title: Britain's Biggest Warship
programme.desc: HMS Queen Elizabeth goes on dangerous sea trials for the first time in the North Sea, where every part of the vessel is put to the test and the first aircraft lands on the deck. After a few days at sea, the first problems start to emerge, including an explosion, a dangerous fuel leak, and some strange noises. Meanwhile, a crew member reveals how the Navy helped him make a fresh start from a life of crime, and the ship's Muslim chef works out how to cook pork without offending his religion(n)

programme.title: Beyond 100 Days
programme.desc: BBC News teams in Washington DC and London report on the events that are shaping the world(n)

programme.title: Pubs, Ponds and Power: The Story of the Village
programme.desc: Archaeologist Ben Robinson explores the history of the Northumberland village of Warkworth. With the help of locals he discovers clues that point back almost 1,000 years to the Norman conquest, revealing how the invaders laid the foundations of a planned community still visible to this day(n)

programme.title: American History's Biggest Fibs with Lucy Worsley
programme.desc: Lucy Worsley debunks the myths behind the American Civil War, revealing how its history has been retold over the years to promote freedom and equality but also hatred and division. She reveals how Abraham Lincoln's personal views, and the behaviour of his troops towards African Americans, were not as noble as they appeared. She also learns how history was rewritten in a bid to downplay the evils of slavery, and how a 1905 blockbuster film about the Civil War relaunched the Ku Klux Klan with terrifying results(n)

programme.title: The Last Battle of the Vikings
programme.desc: Marine archeologist Jon Henderson tells the story of the Vikings in Scotland, visiting sites in Scotland and Norway. He reveals that, although the Battle of Largs in 1263 may have marked the end of an era for the Norsemen, they continued to shape the identity and culture of the nation(n)

programme.title: The Real Doctor Zhivago
programme.desc: Stephen Smith traces the revolutionary beginnings of Boris Pasternak's Doctor Zhivago, a novel that took the author 20 years to write while knowing it could result in his death(n)

programme.title: The Story of China
programme.desc: Michael Wood finds out about China's most creative dynasty, the Song (960-1279). In the city of Kaifeng, he hears the legend of the baby boys who became emperors, then explores the ideas and inventions that made the Song one of greatest eras in world culture. A chef cooks a recipe from a Song cookbook, local women talk about the 1085 volume How To Live Happy Healthy Lives for Old People - which is still in print - and Michael steers a huge working replica of an astronomical clock, made by China's answer to Leonardo da Vinci(n)

programme.title: The Worst Witch
programme.desc: The end of year exams are looming and Maud's annoyed that distracted Mildred isn't taking revision seriously. Miss Cackle goes to the cottage in the woods to learn Agatha has escaped. Ethel is desperate to find out the secret, so she does an impersonation spell to transform into Maud, hoping Mildred will open up to her. Mildred and Enid work out Ethel's plan and tell 'Maud' that the big secret is that Mildred is Miss Cackle's niece. Miss Cackle is annoyed and grounds the two of them. Mildred thinks Miss Cackle isn't herself and worries Agatha had returned from the cottage so she flies to Agatha's cottage in the woods. programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin helps a couple find a home in the Shropshire countryside, and pays a visit to the recently restored 18th-century walled garden at Apley Estate(n)

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: After a successful day of salvaging at the recycling centre in Altrincham, Greater Manchester, upcycling expert Sarah Moore tasks carpenter Norman Wilkinson with reimagining an old wall cabinet. While furniture designer Rupert Blanchard is hoping he can create a top end piece from a pair of metal bench ends, Sarah takes on the challenge of restyling a fashion mannequin(n)

programme.title: Iolo's Street Life
programme.desc: In Iolo Williams' last urban wildlife tour, as the days get shorter, autumn and winter beckon. On a night hunt in Caernarfon he finds spiders that can give a good bite. Stunning birds from Scandinavia drop in at a retail park in Llansamlet, while on an ordinary housing estate in Llangefni on Anglesey, rare red squirrels visit for their winter supply of nuts(n)

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: England cricketer Joe Denly, former striker Chris Sutton, Great Britain sprinter Bianca Williams and Warrington Wolves winger Josh Charnley join team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: The team explores Wembury in Devon, with Anita Rani learning about its naval history, Joe Crowley investigating its rock pools as he takes part in an annual nature survey and Ellie Harrison going in search of its marine wildlife. Elsewhere, Adam Henson is moving some of his sheep and Tom Heap is on the trail of the ramraiding gangs ripping out the ATMs on which villages rely(n)

programme.title: Strictly Come Dancing
programme.desc: Tess Daly and Claudia Winkleman announce the results of the judges' scores and viewers' votes, with the two couples with the lowest combined scores will take part in a dance-off to decide who will be next to leave the contest. Plus, country singer Keith Urban performs Never Comin' Down(n)

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team are in Morden Hall Park in south London, where the experts asses items including a Faberge vanity case, a ring by one of the most innovative goldsmiths of the 20th century and a magnifying glass linked to the Pablo Picasso exhibition in New York in 1939. Lennox Cato faces a puzzle when he appraises a pair of antique doors belonging to the local mosque and jewellery specialist Jon Benjamin gets his hands on the largest aquamarine he has ever seen(n)

programme.title: World on Fire
programme.desc: After enlisting in the Royal Navy, Tom finds himself on board the HMS Exeter in pursuit of German pocket battleship the Graf Spee, and Tom watches the carnage and death around him as a huge naval battle ensues in the South Atlantic. Harry crosses paths with Lois again when she arrives to perform at the BEF base camp in France, while Robina steps in to defend Jan in the playground. In Berlin, Nancy is shocked by rumours of sick children being forcefully taken to clinics and killed and decides to investigate for herself(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day Wales
programme.desc: Wales v Croatia. Jason Mohammad presents highlights of the Euro 2020 Group E qualifier from Cardiff City Stadium, as Ryan Giggs' men looked to avenge a 2-1 defeat in the reverse fixture. With analysis from Kit Symons and Carl Robinson(n)

programme.title: Hindus: Do We Have a Caste Problem?

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Angellica Bell and Matt Baker present the first visit of the week to the One Show studio, featuring the usual mix of topical stories and celebrity chat(n)

programme.title: X-Ray
programme.desc: Omar Hamdi, Lucy Owen and Rachel Treadaway-Williams present an investigation into the private parking industry, and the people who are handing out parking tickets. There is also an interview with the victim of a beauty craze that left her scarred, and a look at the illegal skin cream for sale in Wales(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Kathy suggests to Max that she could supervise while Rainie babysits Abi - but Stuart has a more devious plan up his sleeve. Gray offers to treat Chantelle to an overnight stay in a hotel, while Tiffany tells Keegan she is ready to take their relationship to the next level. Louise and Bex have a heart to heart and Sonia breaks down while talking through her problems with Lisa(n)

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: The Government has promised Britain will radically reduce carbon emissions to achieve net-zero emissions by 2050. To get there, people need to make big changes to the way they live, but how much difference will they really make? With the help of a family in Nottingham, Justin Rowlatt finds out what are the most important things people can do and asks what the government is doing to encourage everyone to alter the way they live(n)

programme.title: Dublin Murders
programme.desc: In the summer of 2006, detectives Rob Reilly and Cassie Maddox are sent to investigate a child's murder, and find a community caught between the old and the new Ireland. Psychological crime drama based on the Dublin Murder Squad book series by Tana French, starring Killian Scott and Sarah Greene, with Ned Dennehy, Conleth Hill and Tom Vaughan-Lawlor(n)

programme.title: The Young Offenders
programme.desc: After the disastrous encounter at the Walsh family barbecue, Conor and Jock reason that there is only one option left to get Linda and Siobhan to really fall for them - spend a whole day together on a beautiful adventure. Meanwhile, Mairead's plan to invite a 'friend' over for dinner doesn't go down well with Conor. Comedy, starring Alex Murphy and Chris Walley(n)

programme.title: Have I Got a Bit More News for You
programme.desc: Doc Martin star Martin Clunes hosts an extended edition of the satirical quiz, with two panellists joining regular team captains Ian Hislop and Paul Merton to poke fun at the week's events(n)

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: Among the guests on the sofa are singer-songwriter Bruce Springsteen, who talks about his new documentary Western Stars, Oscar winner Robert De Niro, starring in Martin Scorsese's The Irishman, and actor and comedian Paul Rudd, who discusses his new TV series Living with Yourself. Music is supplied by James Blunt, who performs Cold, from his new album(n)

programme.desc: Dan Walker and guests look ahead to the forthcoming Euro 2020 qualifiers and review last night's match between the Czech Republic and England(n)

programme.desc: Matt Baker presents live coverage of the penultimate day from Hanns Martin Schleyer Halle in Stuttgart, Germany, featuring the women's vault discipline. With analysis from Beth Tweddle, and commentary by Christine Still and Craig Heap(n)

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman invite another selection of famous faces to take part in the unorthodox general knowledge quiz. programme.title: Strictly Come Dancing
programme.desc: Tess Daly and Claudia Winkleman present the fourth live episode of this year's series. Now that Anneka Rice has departed from the dancefloor, the 13 remaining couples hope to impress the judges and the viewers at home with an array of ballroom and Latin routines. Judges Motsi Mabuse, Craig Revel Horwood, Shirley Ballas and Bruno Tonioli will score the performances out of ten before the vote lines open - which gives viewers at home the chance to have their say and determine who will be leaving the competition(n)

programme.title: The Wall
programme.desc: Game show which sees contestants answer questions that combine knowledge, strategy and luck for the chance to win life-changing prizes. Those taking part won't just require the correct answers, but also the luck of the drop of a single ball, and unflinching trust in one another. In the first episode, sisters Louise and Helen from Leeds face the questions and big decisions, but with a show with so many twists and turns, anything could happen. Hosted by Danny Dyer, with questions voiced by Angela Rippon(n)

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Despite receiving some devastating test results, Duffy continues to offer her support to others, which results in David and Dylan offering her a voluntary position in the emergency department. Meanwhile, Ethan struggles to keep his personal life from distracting him at work, and Archie turns to Will for help(n)

programme.title: Mrs Brown's Boys
programme.desc: Agnes is disappointed to discover she is not invited to Maria's hen party because of her age, and decides to gatecrash the event, only to end up at the wrong venue. She then tries to impress the bride-to-be's mother during a dinner date, but is so nervous she turns to drink, and the evening descends into chaos. Comedy, written by and starring Brendan O'Carroll(n)

programme.title: The Met: Policing London
programme.desc: In 2018 the highest number of under 25s were killed in London in more a decade. This documentary follows the investigation into two of these murders in the borough of Haringey. DCI Luke Marks picks up the case of 22-year-old youth worker Kobi Nelson, who was stabbed near his home and died at the scene. Less than five weeks later, 19-year-old Kelvin Odunuyi is shot dead in Wood Green. Kelvin was a good student although his mother believes he was being groomed by local gangs. Was he killed in direct retaliation for Kobi's murder? It's up to Luke Marks and his team to find out(n)

In the first episode, sisters Louise and Helen from Leeds face the questions and big decisions, but with a show with so many twists and turns, anything could happen. Hosted by Danny Dyer, with questions voiced by Angela Rippon(n)

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Despite receiving some devastating test results, Duffy continues to offer her support to others, which results in David and Dylan offering her a voluntary position in the emergency department. Meanwhile, Ethan struggles to keep his personal life from distracting him at work, and Archie turns to Will for help(n)

programme.title: Mrs Brown's Boys
programme.desc: Agnes is disappointed to discover she is not invited to Maria's hen party because of her age, and decides to gatecrash the event, only to end up at the wrong venue. She then tries to impress the bride-to-be's mother during a dinner date, but is so nervous she turns to drink, and the evening descends into chaos. Comedy, written by and starring Brendan O'Carroll(n)

programme.title: The NFL Show
programme.desc: Mark Chapman is joined by Osi Umenyiora and Jason Bell to present highlights of New England Patriots v New York Giants. Plus, a look at the major stories shaping the NFL world(n)

programme.title: The Client (1994)
programme.desc: A Mafia lawyer tells a boy where a politician's body is buried shortly before committing suicide, prompting a team of district attorneys to press the young witness for information. However, the streetwise kid refuses to give evidence unless his own and his family's safety is guaranteed, and hires a lawyer to ensure his demands are met. Courtroom thriller based on the novel by John Grisham, with Susan Sarandon, Tommy Lee Jones and Brad Renfro(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Ben Thompson and Rachel Burden present a round-up of national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features(n)

programme.title: The Andrew Marr Show
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's stories, featuring interviews with key figures in politics and current affairs and a round-up of the Sunday papers. Including news and weather at 10.00(n)

programme.title: Sunday Politics Northern Ireland
programme.desc: The latest political news and debate, and reports from around the Province, with Mark Carruthers(n)

programme.title: Sunday Morning Live
programme.desc: Sean Fletcher and Ria Hebden present topical debate covering religious, moral and ethical issues, with guest panellists and contributions from viewers(n)

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: Military serviceman Mathew and his fiance Phillipa are ready to leave the UK behind and set off for a trial week in Perth, to sample the lifestyle they could lead if they moved(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Two teams compete at Newark Antiques and Collectors Fair, Nottinghamshire. Ben Cooper and Natasha Raskin are the experts in residence helping the sides in their pursuit for profits, as they go on to sell their items at Charles Hanson's saleroom in Derby. Plus, presenter Paul Laidlaw recounts a tale illustrating how good luck proved as effective as good judgement with the acquisition of a pair of 400-year-old powder flasks(n)

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: Aled Jones travels to the Isle of Wight, with congregational singing from the grounds of Northwood House and two hymns by Cardinal John Newman, to mark the day of his canonisation(n)

programme.title: Points of View
programme.desc: Tina Daheley introduces viewers' opinions on BBC shows and behind-the-scenes reports on new programmes(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts catches up with a development he first saw in Anthorn, Cumbria, in 2012, and the team follows up on projects in Hounslow, west London, and Crewe(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin helps a couple find a home in the Shropshire countryside, and pays a visit to the recently restored 18th-century walled garden at Apley Estate(n)

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich head to Weston-super-Mare, Somerset, where they face an oddly shaped backyard with an assortment of random levels and surfaces(n)

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore transforms items reclaimed from a tip in Altrincham, Greater Manchester - an old leather sofa, a Victorian cabinet and an Arts and Crafts-style bench(n)

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman present a radio-themed edition of the general knowledge quiz, featuring contestants Sarah-Jane Crawford, Dave Berry, Gemma Cairney, Harriet Scott, Liz Kershaw, Tony Livesey, Ken Bruce and Paddy O'Connell(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: The team explores Wembury in Devon, with Anita Rani learning about its naval history, Joe Crowley investigating its rock pools as he takes part in an annual nature survey and Ellie Harrison going in search of its marine wildlife. Elsewhere, Adam Henson is moving some of his sheep and Tom Heap is on the trail of the ramraiding gangs ripping out the ATMs on which villages rely(n)

programme.title: Strictly Come Dancing
programme.desc: Tess Daly and Claudia Winkleman announce the results of the judges' scores and viewers' votes, with the two couples with the lowest combined scores will take part in a dance-off to decide who will be next to leave the contest. Plus, country singer Keith Urban performs Never Comin' Down(n)

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team are in Morden Hall Park in south London, where the experts asses items including a Faberge vanity case, a ring by one of the most innovative goldsmiths of the 20th century and a magnifying glass linked to the Pablo Picasso exhibition in New York in 1939. Lennox Cato faces a puzzle when he appraises a pair of antique doors belonging to the local mosque and jewellery specialist Jon Benjamin gets his hands on the largest aquamarine he has ever seen(n)

programme.title: World on Fire
programme.desc: After enlisting in the Royal Navy, Tom finds himself on board the HMS Exeter in pursuit of German pocket battleship the Graf Spee, and Tom watches the carnage and death around him as a huge naval battle ensues in the South Atlantic. Harry crosses paths with Lois again when she arrives to perform at the BEF base camp in France, while Robina steps in to defend Jan in the playground. In Berlin, Nancy is shocked by rumours of sick children being forcefully taken to clinics and killed and decides to investigate for herself(n)

programme.title: Hindus: Do We Have a Caste Problem?
programme.desc: Parle Patel investigates the hereditary class system of Hindu society, asking what kind of impact does it have on other Hindus living in Britain. He meets a young woman who is so proud of her caste she is still working in the same occupation as her ancestors did thousands of years ago, and also speaks to a man who has rejected his religion because he believes it's the only way to escape his caste(n)

programme.title: The Women's Football Show
programme.desc: Highlights of the weekend's games in the Super League, and a look at the biggest issues in the women's game. Arsenal are the defending champions, but face stiff competition from the likes of Chelsea, Manchester City and newly promoted Manchester United(n)

programme.title: NFL Highlights
programme.desc: Carolina Panthers v Tampa Bay Buccaneers. Action from the NFC South clash, as Tottenham Hotspur Stadium hosted a match for the second successive week(n)

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: England cricketer Joe Denly, former striker Chris Sutton, Great Britain sprinter Bianca Williams and Warrington Wolves winger Josh Charnley join team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Dan Walker and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.title: Rip Off Britain: Live
programme.desc: Angela Rippon, Gloria Hunniford and Julia Somerville return with a ive week of the consumer series, reporting on people who have lost tens of thousands of pounds in dormant accounts that banks say are untraceable(n)

programme.title: Caught Red Handed
programme.desc: Dominic Littlewood returns to reveal more of the innovative methods that police officers and members of the public are using to catch criminals in the act. A neighbourly dispute gets out of hand, a shop worker fears for his life when two violent men burst in, and a thief goes into a pet shop and steals a puppy(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Charlie Ross presents from the Royal Cornwall Showground in Wadebridge as Thomas Forrester and Tim Weeks help two teams look for items to sell at auction in Plymouth(n)

programme.title: BBC Newsline; Weather
programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland(n)

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Imogen struggles to open up about her health fears, Ruhma bares her soul on camera, and Sid finds the key to helping a patient lies in a controversial painting and a family secret(n)

programme.title: Head Hunters
programme.desc: Rob Beckett hosts the quiz in which contestants must choose between working together to combine their knowledge or going it alone to claim the prize for themselves(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Perfect Town
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin puts Tunbridge Wells and its property market to the test with his first- time buyers, who have £425,000 to invest in a starter home after saving hard for the deposit(n)

programme.title: Make Me a Dealer
programme.desc: Paul Martin is in Dorset as driving instructor Helen Adams and former flight attendant Yvonne Johnson use their own cash to buy and sell antiques to try to make the most profit(n)

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore salvages three items from a recycling centre in Witley, with Leigh-Anne Treadwell and Neil Wragg helping transform a sofa, some deckchairs and two tables(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Angellica Bell and Matt Baker present the first visit of the week to the One Show studio, featuring the usual mix of topical stories and celebrity chat(n)

programme.title: Parents' Evening
programme.desc: A look at the Year 9 pupils at St Kevin's College in Lisnaskea, Fermanagh, finding out if their grades are up to scratch and whether hormonal changes are getting the best of them(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Kathy suggests to Max that she could supervise while Rainie babysits Abi - but Stuart has a more devious plan up his sleeve. Gray offers to treat Chantelle to an overnight stay in a hotel, while Tiffany tells Keegan she is ready to take their relationship to the next level. Louise and Bex have a heart to heart and Sonia breaks down while talking through her problems with Lisa(n)

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: The Government has promised Britain will radically reduce carbon emissions to achieve net-zero emissions by 2050. To get there, people need to make big changes to the way they live, but how much difference will they really make? With the help of a family in Nottingham, Justin Rowlatt finds out what are the most important things people can do and asks what the government is doing to encourage everyone to alter the way they live(n)

programme.title: Dublin Murders
programme.desc: In the summer of 2006, detectives Rob Reilly and Cassie Maddox are sent to investigate a child's murder, and find a community caught between the old and the new Ireland. Psychological crime drama based on the Dublin Murder Squad book series by Tana French, starring Killian Scott and Sarah Greene, with Ned Dennehy, Conleth Hill and Tom Vaughan-Lawlor(n)

programme.title: True North
programme.desc: The work of Bangor barber Lenny White, who cuts the hair of men with dementia and offers them his unique form of therapy. The documentary shows Lenny meeting a wide range of people living with different forms of the disease, from Alzheimer's - the most common - through to the much rarer early-onset dementia(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day from Northern Ireland
programme.desc: Czech Republic v Northern Ireland. Stephen Watson presents action from the friendly at Generali Arena in Prague. With analysis from John O'Neill and commentary by Thomas Kane(n)

programme.title: Pop Goes Northern Ireland
programme.desc: Music and news footage from 1980, the year a government constitutional conference set up to find a solution to the political conflict in Northern Ireland ended in failure following the refusal of the Ulster Unionist Party to take part. Elsewhere, Republican prisoners at the Maze prison began a hunger strike(n)

programme.desc: Mark D'Arcy talks to Ian Murray about his new book 'This is Our Story', on the fight to save Heart of Midlothian F.C. from liquidation(n)

programme.title: Briefings
programme.desc: The speech by Labour leader Jeremy Corbyn in Northampton ahead of next Monday's Queens Speech. Mr Corbyn set out Labour's plans for transforming the UK in the case of any forthcoming general election, from Thursday 10 October(n)

programme.desc: International Development Committee's session on the humanitarian situations in Yemen, Syria, and Israel and the Occupied Palestinian Territories with evidence from Minister for the Middle East Andrew Murrison, from Tuesday 8 October(n)

programme.title: HARDtalk
programme.desc: Brexit represents a political gamble played for the highest of stakes. If Britain leaves the EU without a deal, there will be significant economic disruption; even the most ardent Brexiteers acknowledge that. But they believe the potential rewards justify the risk. Stephen Sackur speaks to Stuart Wheeler, a successful businessman and lifelong gambler, who backed his commitment to Brexit with plenty of his own cash. Has his money given him undue influence over Britain's future?(n)

programme.desc: Topical Questions to Ministers in the Welsh Assembly, from Wednesday 9 October.(n)

programme.title: Select Committees
programme.desc: Full coverage of the Public Administration and Constitutional Affairs select committee session on the Supreme Court's judgment on the government's decision to suspend Parliament for five weeks ahead of the Queen's speech which was found to be unlawful, with evidence from Lord Sumption, former Supreme Court Justice as well as academics, from Tuesday 8 October(n)

programme.title: Select Committees
programme.desc: Home Affairs Committee's session on Home Office preparations for Brexit with evidence from Shanker Singham, Technical Panel, and Tony Smith, Alternative Arrangements Commission and then from Sam Lowe, Centre for European Reform, and Dr Anna Jerzewska, Customs and Trade Consultant, from Tuesday 8 October.(n)

programme.title: RWC 2019 Live
programme.desc: Ireland v Samoa (Kick-off 11.45am). Craig Doyle presents all the action from both teams' fourth and concluding Pool A fixture, held at Fukuoka Hakatanomori Stadium in Japan. Ireland entered the tournament as the favourites to top the pool, but will be wary of complacency after their shock defeat against the hosts. With analysis from Brian O'Driscoll, Paul O'Connell and Ian McGeechan, and commentary by Miles Harrison and Alan Quinlan(n)

programme.title: ITV Racing Live
programme.desc: Ed Chamberlin and Francesca Cumani present coverage of Future Champions' Day at Newmarket, including the 3.30 Darley Dewhurst Stakes and 4.10 Dubai Cesarewitch Stakes, which follow races at 2.20 and 2.55. Plus, races at 2.40, 3.15 and 3.50 from York. With analysis from Jason Weaver and Johnny Murtagh, reports by Oli Bell and Matt Chapman, and commentary from Richard Hoiles(n)

programme.title: Paul O'Grady's Little Heroes
programme.desc: Paul meets 13-year-old Noah, who has checked into the neurology ward ahead of an operation to correct a structural defect of the skull. The youngster needs to overcome his anxiety, however, so Paul joins him on a fun tour aimed at demystifying surgical theatres. Other children include seven-year-old Oliver, a long-term patient who is struggling to walk after being struck down by arthritis, and 17-year-old Rue, who has lost much of her sight and is now using various gadgets to re-learn many everyday tasks(n)

programme.title: You've Been Framed!

programme.title: Catchphrase

After the first elimination, the two remaining players battle to reach the Super Catchphrase round, where one of them could win £50,000 for their chosen charity(n)

programme.title: The Chase Celebrity Special
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as TV presenters Donna Air, Amanda Lamb, Sarah Greene and Dan Snow pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds for charity. They must work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught(n)

programme.title: The X Factor: Celebrity
programme.desc: Dermot O'Leary hosts a star-studded version of the singing contest, with judges Simon Cowell, Nicole Scherzinger and Louis Walsh mentoring the famous faces. The series kicks off at Simon's home in Los Angeles, where the celebs are split into three categories - Overs, Unders and Groups. Representing the Overs are journalist Martin Bashir, talk show host Ricki Lake, Chaser Jenny 'The Vixen' Ryan and actress Victoria Ekanoye. Reality star Megan McKenna, model and actress Hayley Hasselhoff, soap star Jonny Labey, film star Olivia Olson and actor Kevin McHale are the Unders, while the groups consist of Love Island's Samira Mighty, Zara McDermott, Wes Nelson and Eyal Booker, sports stars Ben Foden, Thom Evans and Levi Davis, dancer Brendan Cole and actor Jeremy Edwards, CBBC presenters Max and Harvey, and influencers Sofia, Alondra, Laura, Wendii and Natalie(n)

programme.title: The Jonathan Ross Show
programme.desc: The host is joined on the sofa by renowned chef Gordon Ramsay, Team GB sprinter Dina Asher-Smith and stand-up comedian Rob Beckett. Plus, multi-platinum-selling Irish group the Script perform live in the studio(n)

programme.title: A Confession
programme.desc: Karen takes her fight for justice all the way to Downing Street, but it proves fruitless - the chance of seeing her daughter's killer stand trial is seemingly impossible. Fulcher's scandalous actions cost him his position in the police and see him forced to leave the country, only able to find work as a security consultant in African hotspots. However, fresh hope arises when Wiltshire Police find a way to possibly bring Christopher Halliwell to court for Becky's murder - but the disgraced detective must return home to face the man who ruined his career(n)

programme.title: Rugby World Cup 2019 Highlights

programme.title: Sugar Free Farm
programme.desc: Now on day four of the sugar free diet, the celebrities are settling into the farm work but there is a tantrum over chickpeas in the pig field and a heated debate over dinner. Nutritionist Angelique Panagos tries to persuade them of the positives of a life without sugar, but even after a reality check at the hospital, Mark 'The Beast' Labbett is still reluctant to listen. Will the familiar faces find the going too tough as they do without sweet treats, or will they go the distance? Olivia Colman narrates(n)

programme.title: Tipping Point

Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot(n)

programme.title: Harry Potter and the Chamber of Secrets (2002)

programme.title: Sanditon

On the night of the Midsummer Ball, romantic interests are finally resolved. programme.title: Inside Prison: Britain Behind Bars
programme.desc: The documentary lifting the lid on life behind bars focuses on HMP Isle of Wight, a specialist prison housing many sex offenders and paedophiles. Intelligence has been gathered that a USB stick containing child pornography is doing the rounds, so the officers launch a search. But finding such a small item in a vast prison is no easy task. The staff also face a constant battle against drugs, with the inmates finding increasingly sophisticated and creative ways to smuggle them in, from letters sprayed with a spice solution to hollowed-out chess pieces(n)

programme.title: Live International Football
programme.desc: Bulgaria v England (Kick-off 7.45pm). Mark Pougatch presents all the action from the Group A qualifier at Vasil Levski National Stadium in Sofia. England recorded a comfortable 4-0 victory in September's reverse fixture, with Harry Kane scoring a hat-trick that included two penalties and Raheem Sterling also finding the net. The match summed up the qualifying campaign as a whole, with Gareth Southgate's men in dominant form and the Bulgarians struggling for points, but Krasimir Balakov will be hoping his side can produce a stronger display in front of their own fans. With analysis from Ian Wright, Roy Keane and Lee Dixon, and commentary by Clive Tyldesley and Glenn Hoddle(n)

programme.title: International Football Highlights
programme.desc: Bulgaria v England. Mark Pougatch presents action from the Group A qualifier at Vasil Levski National Stadium in Sofia. With analysis from Glenn Hoddle and Lee Dixon(n)

programme.desc: Ireland v Samoa (Kick-off 11.45am). Craig Doyle presents all the action from both teams' fourth and concluding Pool A fixture, held at Fukuoka Hakatanomori Stadium in Japan. programme.title: ITV Racing Live
programme.desc: Ed Chamberlin and Francesca Cumani present coverage of Future Champions' Day at Newmarket, including the 3.30 Darley Dewhurst Stakes and 4.10 Dubai Cesarewitch Stakes, which follow races at 2.20 and 2.55. programme.title: Paul O'Grady's Little Heroes
programme.desc: Paul meets 13-year-old Noah, who has checked into the neurology ward ahead of an operation to correct a structural defect of the skull. The youngster needs to overcome his anxiety, however, so Paul joins him on a fun tour aimed at demystifying surgical theatres. Other children include seven-year-old Oliver, a long-term patient who is struggling to walk after being struck down by arthritis, and 17-year-old Rue, who has lost much of her sight and is now using various gadgets to re-learn many everyday tasks(n)

programme.title: The X Factor: Celebrity
programme.desc: Dermot O'Leary hosts a star-studded version of the singing contest, with judges Simon Cowell, Nicole Scherzinger and Louis Walsh mentoring the famous faces. The series kicks off at Simon's home in Los Angeles, where the celebs are split into three categories - Overs, Unders and Groups. Representing the Overs are journalist Martin Bashir, talk show host Ricki Lake, Chaser Jenny 'The Vixen' Ryan and actress Victoria Ekanoye. Reality star Megan McKenna, model and actress Hayley Hasselhoff, soap star Jonny Labey, film star Olivia Olson and actor Kevin McHale are the Unders, while the groups consist of Love Island's Samira Mighty, Zara McDermott, Wes Nelson and Eyal Booker, sports stars Ben Foden, Thom Evans and Levi Davis, dancer Brendan Cole and actor Jeremy Edwards, CBBC presenters Max and Harvey, and influencers Sofia, Alondra, Laura, Wendii and Natalie(n)

programme.title: The Jonathan Ross Show
programme.desc: The host is joined on the sofa by renowned chef Gordon Ramsay, Team GB sprinter Dina Asher-Smith and stand-up comedian Rob Beckett. Plus, multi-platinum-selling Irish group the Script perform live in the studio(n)

programme.title: A Confession
programme.desc: Karen takes her fight for justice all the way to Downing Street, but it proves fruitless - the chance of seeing her daughter's killer stand trial is seemingly impossible. Fulcher's scandalous actions cost him his position in the police and see him forced to leave the country, only able to find work as a security consultant in African hotspots. However, fresh hope arises when Wiltshire Police find a way to possibly bring Christopher Halliwell to court for Becky's murder - but the disgraced detective must return home to face the man who ruined his career(n)

programme.title: Rugby World Cup 2019 Highlights

programme.title: Sugar Free Farm
programme.desc: Now on day four of the sugar free diet, the celebrities are settling into the farm work but there is a tantrum over chickpeas in the pig field and a heated debate over dinner. Nutritionist Angelique Panagos tries to persuade them of the positives of a life without sugar, but even after a reality check at the hospital, Mark 'The Beast' Labbett is still reluctant to listen. Will the familiar faces find the going too tough as they do without sweet treats, or will they go the distance? Olivia Colman narrates(n)

programme.title: Inside Prison: Britain Behind Bars
programme.desc: The documentary lifting the lid on life behind bars focuses on HMP Isle of Wight, a specialist prison housing many sex offenders and paedophiles. Intelligence has been gathered that a USB stick containing child pornography is doing the rounds, so the officers launch a search. But finding such a small item in a vast prison is no easy task. The staff also face a constant battle against drugs, with the inmates finding increasingly sophisticated and creative ways to smuggle them in, from letters sprayed with a spice solution to hollowed-out chess pieces(n)
